title: Profile icon account creation flow with user dashboard stats

requirements.summary: Guide users through account creation flow when clicking the profile icon if no profile exists, then display dashboard after successful profile creation
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the profile icon when no profile exists initiates the account creation / onboarding flow
The user cannot access the dashboard until they have completed account creation
Clicking the profile icon when a profile already exists goes directly to the dashboard
The onboarding flow includes Internet Identity login, optional Google Sign-In for Gmail linking, and profile details entry
On successful profile creation, the user is transitioned to the dashboard view

file_operations.description: Refactor ProfileView to implement a multi-step onboarding flow when no profile exists: (1) ensure Internet Identity authentication, (2) present optional Google Sign-In for Gmail linking, (3) display profile details form (name entry), (4) on successful profile save, transition to the dashboard section. When a profile already exists, skip directly to the dashboard. Use the authorization component's getCallerUserProfile to check profile existence and saveCallerUserProfile/createUserProfileWithGoogle for profile creation. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update the profile button click handler in HubTopBar to set the view state to 'profile' without any additional guards. The ProfileView will handle the onboarding flow internally based on profile existence. Ensure the profile icon remains accessible to all authenticated users.

requirements.summary: Add user dashboard section within ProfileView displaying stat cards for Tasks Completed, User Rating, Total Ratings, and Tasks Posted, fetched from the user's backend Profile record
acceptanceCriteria: The dashboard displays a 'Tasks Completed' stat card with the correct count
The dashboard displays a 'User Rating' stat card showing the average star rating (e.g., 4.5 ★)
The dashboard displays a 'Total Ratings' stat card with the total number of ratings received
The dashboard displays a 'Tasks Posted' stat card with the correct count
All stats are loaded from the backend Profile data for the authenticated user
A loading skeleton is shown while data is being fetched
Stat cards use the existing PROXIIS neon/dark visual theme with the green-to-purple gradient accents

file_operations.description: Add a new React Query hook useGetCallerProfileStats that fetches the caller's Profile (containing tasksCompleted, averageRating, totalRatingsCount, and tasksPosted fields) from the backend via the getCallerProfile backend capability. Return the fetched Profile data along with loading and error states. Use the authorization component's data access patterns. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a new DashboardStatsPanel component that uses the useGetCallerProfileStats hook to fetch and display four stat cards: 'Tasks Completed' (tasksCompleted count), 'User Rating' (averageRating formatted as stars, e.g., '4.5 ★'), 'Total Ratings' (totalRatingsCount), and 'Tasks Posted' (tasksPosted count). Show loading skeletons while data is being fetched. Style the stat cards with the PROXIIS neon/dark theme using the green-to-purple gradient accents for highlights and borders. Use existing Tailwind utilities and design tokens from frontend/tailwind.config.js and frontend/src/index.css.

After the onboarding flow is complete and a profile exists, render the DashboardStatsPanel component within the ProfileView dashboard section. Position it above the existing task post history and rating sections. Ensure the dashboard only displays when the user has a valid profile and is authenticated.
